claim_text: Anthropic published a scoped sabotage risk report for Claude Opus 4.6 and framed the overall risk as very low but not negligible.
source_string: overall risk is very low but not negligible

source.title: Sabotage Risk Report: Claude Opus 4.6
source.source_string: overall risk is very low but not negligible

non_claims: Does not prove all Anthropic models are safe.
Does not evaluate threat models centered on intentional harmful human action.
Does not independently validate redacted evidence.

claim_text: OpenAI treats GPT-5.5 Instant as High capability in Cybersecurity and Biological and Chemical Preparedness, with deployment safeguards and a reasoning-effort boundary.
source_string: first Instant model to be treated as High Capability

source.title: GPT-5.5 Instant System Card
source.source_string: first Instant model to be treated as High Capability

non_claims: Does not prove an application using the model is safe.
Does not prove deployed low-reasoning-effort behavior equals xhigh evaluation behavior.
Does not prove customer-specific safeguards are configured correctly.

claim_text: Microsoft describes NCII controls covering real and synthetic content, reporting improvements, hash matching, human review for reported cases, and appeals.
source_string: maintaining human review for reported cases

source.title: Strengthening our approach to tackling non-consensual intimate imagery
source.source_string: maintaining human review for reported cases

non_claims: Does not prove every NCII report is correctly handled.
Does not prove actual removal within a specific SLA unless timestamped case evidence is present.
Does not certify compliance with any law.

claim_text: The European Commission's draft guidance structures assessment of high-risk AI classification under Article 6 and Annex III.
source_string: classification purposes and, in accordance with Article 6(5)

source.title: Draft Commission guidelines on the classification of high-risk AI systems
source.source_string: classification purposes and, in accordance with Article 6(5)

non_claims: Does not certify EU AI Act compliance.
Does not replace provider, deployer, or regulator classification duties.

claim_text: FTC enforcement against Active Listening claims shows that AI marketing claims and opt-in representations require substantiation.
source_string: falsely claiming to offer an AI-powered service

source.title: FTC to Require Cox Media Group, Two Other Firms to Pay Nearly $1 Million...
source.source_string: falsely claiming to offer an AI-powered service

non_claims: Does not prove the marketed service uses conversation data.
Does not prove opt-in consent exists.
Does not permit public claims beyond the supplied evidence.
